I’m narrowing from broad `group_id` noise into production contracts: Django relations, serializers/API surfaces, task payloads, frontend typed issue objects, and less obvious subsystems like replays, monitors, workflows, and Seer/autofix.

    88	def parse_short_id(short_id_s: str) -> ShortId | None:
    89	    match = _short_id_re.match(short_id_s.strip())
    90	    if match is None:
    91	        return None
    92	    slug, id = match.groups()
    93	    slug = slug.lower()
    94	    try:
    95	        short_id = base32_decode(id)
    96	        # We need to make sure the short id is not overflowing the
    97	        # field's max or the lookup will fail with an assertion error.
    98	        max_id = Group._meta.get_field("short_id").MAX_VALUE
    99	        if short_id > max_id:
   100	            return None
   101	    except ValueError:
   102	        return None
   103	    return ShortId(slug, short_id)

   110	def get_group_with_redirect(
   111	    id_or_qualified_short_id: int | str,
   112	    queryset: BaseQuerySet[Group, Group] | None = None,
   113	    organization: Organization | None = None,
   114	) -> tuple[Group, bool]:
   115	    """
   116	    Retrieve a group by ID, checking the redirect table if the requested group
   117	    does not exist. Returns a two-tuple of ``(object, redirected)``.
   118	    """
   119	    if queryset is None:
   120	        if organization:
   121	            updated_qs = Group.objects.filter(project__organization=organization)
   122	            getter = updated_qs.get
   123	        else:
   124	            updated_qs = Group.objects.all()
   125	            # When not passing a queryset, we want to read from cache
   126	            getter = Group.objects.get_from_cache
   127	    else:
   128	        if organization:
   129	            updated_qs = queryset.filter(project__organization=organization)
   130	        else:
   131	            # No updates!
   132	            updated_qs = queryset
   133	        getter = updated_qs.get
   134	
   135	    if not (isinstance(id_or_qualified_short_id, int) or id_or_qualified_short_id.isdigit()):
   136	        short_id = parse_short_id(id_or_qualified_short_id)
   137	        if not short_id or not organization:
   138	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   139	        params = {
   140	            "project__slug": short_id.project_slug,
   141	            "short_id": short_id.short_id,
   142	            "project__organization": organization,
   143	        }
   144	    else:
   145	        short_id = None
   146	        # Validate that the numeric ID doesn't exceed the max value for the
   147	        # bounded field, otherwise the ORM will raise an AssertionError.
   148	        max_id = Group._meta.get_field("id").MAX_VALUE
   149	        if int(id_or_qualified_short_id) > max_id:
   150	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   151	        params = {"id": id_or_qualified_short_id}
   152	
   153	    try:
   154	        return getter(**params), False
   155	    except Group.DoesNotExist as error:
   156	        from sentry.models.groupredirect import GroupRedirect
   157	
   158	        if short_id:
   159	            # Known because we only set short_id in a path that raises if org is None
   160	            assert organization is not None
   161	            params = {
   162	                "id__in": GroupRedirect.objects.filter(
   163	                    organization_id=organization.id,
   164	                    previous_short_id=short_id.short_id,
   165	                    previous_project_slug=short_id.project_slug,
   166	                ).values_list("group_id", flat=True)[:1]
   167	            }
   168	        else:
   169	            params = {
   170	                "id__in": GroupRedirect.objects.filter(
   171	                    previous_group_id=id_or_qualified_short_id,
   172	                ).values_list("group_id", flat=True)[:1]
   173	            }
   174	
   175	        try:
   176	            return updated_qs.get(**params), True
   177	        except Group.DoesNotExist:
   178	            raise error  # raise original `DoesNotExist`

   402	// Occurrence type IDs for hidden issue types - used to filter API queries.
   403	// Note: This only works for issuePlatform events not discover/error events.
   404	export const HIDDEN_OCCURRENCE_TYPE_IDS: number[] = Object.entries(
   405	  OCCURRENCE_TYPE_TO_ISSUE_TYPE
   406	)
   407	  .filter(([_, issueType]) => HIDDEN_ISSUE_TYPES.includes(issueType))
   408	  .map(([id]) => Number(id));
   409	
   410	const PERFORMANCE_REGRESSION_TYPE_IDS = new Set([1017, 1018, 2010, 2011]);
   411	
   412	export function getIssueTypeFromOccurrenceType(
   413	  typeId: number | undefined
   414	): IssueType | null {
   415	  if (!typeId) {
   416	    return null;
   417	  }
   418	  // @ts-expect-error TS(7053): Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expre... Remove this comment to see the full error message
   419	  return OCCURRENCE_TYPE_TO_ISSUE_TYPE[typeId] ?? null;
   420	}
   421	
   422	export function isTransactionBased(typeId: number | undefined): boolean {
   423	  if (!typeId) {
   424	    return false;
   425	  }
   426	  // the 1xxx type ids are transaction based performance issues
   427	  return typeId >= 1000 && typeId < 2000;
   428	}
   429	
   430	export function isOccurrenceBased(typeId: number | undefined): boolean {
   431	  if (!typeId) {
   432	    return false;
   433	  }
   434	  // these are regression type performance issues
   435	  return !PERFORMANCE_REGRESSION_TYPE_IDS.has(typeId);
   436	}

1	"""
     2	Group deletion configuration.
     3	
     4	This module defines which models should be deleted when a group is deleted and how
     5	they should be handled during reprocessing operations.
     6	
     7	IMPORTANT: When adding a new model with a group_id foreign key, you MUST add it to
     8	one of the model lists below, or tests will fail. See:
     9	  - tests/sentry/deletions/test_validate_group_related_models.py
    10	
    11	For guidance on which list to use, see the comments on DIRECT_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS
    12	and ADDITIONAL_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS below.
    13	"""
    14	
    15	from __future__ import annotations
    16	
    17	import logging
    18	import os
    19	from collections import defaultdict
    20	from collections.abc import Mapping, Sequence
    21	from typing import Any
    22	
    23	from sentry import models, options
    24	from sentry.deletions.tasks.nodestore import delete_events_for_groups_from_nodestore_and_eventstore
    25	from sentry.issues.grouptype import GroupCategory, InvalidGroupTypeError
    26	from sentry.issues.models.groupactionlogentry import GroupActionLogEntry
    27	from sentry.issues.models.groupderiveddata import GroupDerivedData
    28	from sentry.models.group import Group, GroupStatus
    29	from sentry.models.grouphash import GroupHash
    30	from sentry.models.grouphashmetadata import GroupHashMetadata
    31	from sentry.notifications.models.notificationmessage import NotificationMessage
    32	from sentry.seer.models.night_shift import SeerNightShiftRunResult
    33	from sentry.services.eventstore.models import Event
    34	from sentry.snuba.dataset import Dataset
    35	from sentry.tasks.seer.delete_seer_grouping_records import (
    36	    may_schedule_task_to_delete_hashes_from_seer,
    37	)
    38	from sentry.utils import metrics
    39	
    40	from ..base import BaseDeletionTask, BaseRelation, ModelDeletionTask, ModelRelation
    41	from ..manager import DeletionTaskManager
    42	
    43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    44	
    45	GROUP_CHUNK_SIZE = 100
    46	EVENT_CHUNK_SIZE = 10000
    47	GROUP_HASH_ITERATIONS = 10000
    48	GROUP_HASH_METADATA_ITERATIONS = 10000
    49	
    50	# Group models that relate only to groups and not to events. These models are
    51	# transferred during reprocessing operations because they represent group-level
    52	# metadata that should follow the group when events are reprocessed.
    53	#
    54	# Add a model to this list if it meets ALL of these criteria:
    55	# 1. NO event_id field - represents group state, not individual event data
    56	# 2. Should be TRANSFERRED during reprocessing - the metadata is about the group itself
    57	#    (e.g., assignments, bookmarks, resolutions, history) and should move with it
    58	# 3. Safe to bulk update with group_id changes during merge/unmerge operations
    59	DIRECT_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS = (
    60	    # prioritize GroupHash
    61	    # XXX: We could remove GroupHash from here since we call delete_group_hashes() in the _delete_children() method.
    62	    models.GroupHash,
    63	    models.GroupAssignee,
    64	    models.GroupCommitResolution,
    65	    models.GroupLink,
    66	    models.GroupHistory,
    67	    models.GroupBookmark,
    68	    models.GroupMeta,
    69	    models.GroupEnvironment,
    70	    models.GroupOpenPeriod,
    71	    models.GroupRelease,
    72	    models.GroupRedirect,
    73	    models.GroupResolution,
    74	    models.GroupRuleStatus,
    75	    models.GroupSeen,
    76	    models.GroupShare,
    77	    models.GroupSnooze,
    78	    models.GroupInbox,
    79	    models.GroupOwner,
    80	    models.GroupEmailThread,
    81	    models.GroupSubscription,
    82	    models.GroupReaction,
    83	    models.Activity,
    84	    GroupActionLogEntry,
    85	    GroupDerivedData,
    86	)
    87	
    88	# Additional group-related models that require special handling during reprocessing.
    89	# Unlike DIRECT_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS which are migrated in bulk, these models need
    90	# per-event processing or should not be transferred at all.
    91	#
    92	# Add a model to this list if it meets ANY of these criteria:
    93	# 1. Has an event_id field - per-event data that must be migrated during event
    94	#    reprocessing pipeline, not as a group bulk operation (UserReport, EventAttachment)
    95	# 2. Should NOT be transferred during reprocessing - transient or notification data
    96	#    that doesn't represent core group state (NotificationMessage)
    97	ADDITIONAL_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS = (
    98	    models.UserReport,
    99	    models.EventAttachment,
   100	    NotificationMessage,
   101	    SeerNightShiftRunResult,
   102	)
   103	_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS = DIRECT_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS + ADDITIONAL_GROUP_RELATED_MODELS
   104	
   105	
   106	class EventsBaseDeletionTask(BaseDeletionTask[Group]):
   107	    """
   108	    Base class to delete events associated to groups and its related models.
   109	    """
   110	
   111	    # Number of events fetched from eventstore per chunk() call.
   112	    DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE = EVENT_CHUNK_SIZE
   113	    referrer = "deletions.group"
   114	    dataset: Dataset
   115	
   116	    def __init__(
   117	        self, manager: DeletionTaskManager, groups: Sequence[Group], **kwargs: Any
   118	    ) -> None:
   119	        self.groups = groups
   120	        # Use self.last_event to keep track of the last event processed in the chunk method.
   121	        self.last_event: Event | None = None
   122	        self.set_group_and_project_ids()
   123	        super().__init__(manager, **kwargs)
   124	
   125	    def set_group_and_project_ids(self) -> None:
   126	        group_ids = []
   127	        self.project_groups: defaultdict[int, list[Group]] = defaultdict(list)
   128	        for group in self.groups:
   129	            self.project_groups[group.project_id].append(group)
   130	            group_ids.append(group.id)
   131	        self.group_ids = group_ids
   132	        self.project_ids = list(self.project_groups.keys())
   133	
   134	    @property
   135	    def tenant_ids(self) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
   136	        result = {"referrer": self.referrer}
   137	        if self.groups:
   138	            result["organization_id"] = self.groups[0].project.organization_id
   139	        return result
   140	
   141	    def chunk(self, apply_filter: bool = False) -> bool:
   142	        """This method is called to delete chunks of data. It returns a boolean to say
   143	        if the deletion has completed and if it needs to be called again."""
   144	        self.delete_events_from_nodestore_and_eventstore()
   145	        return False
   146	
   147	    def delete_events_from_nodestore_and_eventstore(self) -> None:
   148	        """Schedule asynchronous deletion of events from the nodestore and eventstore for all groups."""
   149	        if not self.group_ids:
   150	            return
   151	
   152	        # Get organization_id from the first group
   153	        organization_id = self.groups[0].project.organization_id
   154	
   155	        # Schedule nodestore deletion task for each project
   156	        for project_id, groups in self.project_groups.items():
   157	            sorted_groups = sorted(groups, key=lambda g: (g.times_seen, g.id))
   158	            sorted_group_ids = [group.id for group in sorted_groups]
   159	            sorted_times_seen = [group.times_seen for group in sorted_groups]
   160	            # The scheduled task will not have access to the Group model, thus, we need to pass the times_seen
   161	            # in order to enable proper batching and calling deletions with less than ISSUE_PLATFORM_MAX_ROWS_TO_DELETE
   162	            delete_events_for_groups_from_nodestore_and_eventstore.apply_async(
   163	                kwargs={
   164	                    "organization_id": organization_id,
   165	                    "project_id": project_id,
   166	                    "group_ids": sorted_group_ids,
   167	                    "times_seen": sorted_times_seen,
   168	                    "transaction_id": self.transaction_id,
   169	                    "dataset_str": self.dataset.value,
   170	                    "referrer": self.referrer,
   171	                },
   172	            )
   173	
   174	
   175	class ErrorEventsDeletionTask(EventsBaseDeletionTask):
   176	    """
   177	    Deletes nodestore data, EventAttachment and UserReports for requested groups.
   178	
   179	    This class uses the old Snuba deletion method.
   180	    """

   201	def _get_status_label(group: Group):
   202	    status = group.get_status()
   203	
   204	    if status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   205	        status_label = "resolved"
   206	    elif status == GroupStatus.IGNORED:
   207	        status_label = "ignored"
   208	    elif status in [GroupStatus.PENDING_DELETION, GroupStatus.DELETION_IN_PROGRESS]:
   209	        status_label = "pending_deletion"
   210	    elif status == GroupStatus.PENDING_MERGE:
   211	        status_label = "pending_merge"
   212	    elif status == GroupStatus.REPROCESSING:
   213	        status_label = "reprocessing"
   214	    else:
   215	        status_label = "unresolved"
   216	
   217	    return status_label
   218

   229	    def __init__(

   237	    def _serialize_assignees(self, item_list: Sequence[Group]) -> Mapping[int, Team | Any]:
   238	        gas = GroupAssignee.objects.filter(group__in=item_list)
   239	        result: MutableMapping[int, Team | Any] = {}
   240	        all_team_ids: MutableMapping[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
   241	        all_user_ids: MutableMapping[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
   242	
   243	        for g in gas:
   244	            if g.team_id:
   245	                all_team_ids[g.team_id].add(g.group_id)
   246	            if g.user_id:
   247	                all_user_ids[g.user_id].add(g.group_id)
   248	
   249	        for team in Team.objects.filter(id__in=all_team_ids.keys()):
   250	            for group_id in all_team_ids[team.id]:
   251	                result[group_id] = team
   252	
   253	        user_ids = list(all_user_ids.keys())
   254	        if user_ids:
   255	            for user in user_service.get_many_by_id(ids=user_ids):
   256	                for group_id in all_user_ids[user.id]:
   257	                    result[group_id] = user
   258	
   259	        return result
   260

   261	    def get_attrs(
   262	        self, item_list: Sequence[Group], user: User | RpcUser | AnonymousUser, **kwargs: Any
   263	    ) -> dict[Group, dict[str, Any]]:
   264	        GroupMeta.objects.populate_cache(item_list)
   265	
   266	        # Note that organization is necessary here for use in `_get_permalink` to avoid
   267	        # making unnecessary queries.
   268	        prefetch_related_objects(item_list, "project__organization")
   269	
   270	        if user.is_authenticated and item_list:
   271	            bookmarks = set(
   272	                GroupBookmark.objects.filter(user_id=user.id, group__in=item_list).values_list(
   273	                    "group_id", flat=True
   274	                )
   275	            )
   276	            seen_groups = dict(
   277	                GroupSeen.objects.filter(user_id=user.id, group__in=item_list).values_list(
   278	                    "group_id", "last_seen"
   279	                )
   280	            )
   281	            subscriptions = self._get_subscriptions(item_list, user)
   282	        else:
   283	            bookmarks = set()
   284	            seen_groups = {}
   285	            subscriptions = defaultdict(lambda: (False, False, None))
   286	
   287	        resolved_assignees = self._serialize_assignees(item_list)
   288	
   289	        ignore_items = {g.group_id: g for g in GroupSnooze.objects.filter(group__in=item_list)}
   290	
   291	        release_resolutions, commit_resolutions = self._resolve_resolutions(item_list, user)
   292	
   293	        user_ids = {
   294	            user_id
   295	            for user_id in itertools.chain(
   296	                (r[-1] for r in release_resolutions.values()),
   297	                (r.actor_id for r in ignore_items.values()),
   298	            )
   299	            if user_id is not None
   300	        }
   301	        if user_ids:
   302	            serialized_users = user_service.serialize_many(
   303	                filter={"user_ids": user_ids, "is_active": True},
   304	                as_user=serialize_generic_user(user),
   305	            )
   306	            # `serialize_many` may omit user_ids that are inactive or missing in
   307	            # the control silo; key by the returned id rather than zipping so
   308	            # drifted users don't silently misalign with the request ordering.
   309	            actors = {int(u["id"]): u for u in serialized_users}
   310	        else:
   311	            actors = {}
   312	
   313	        share_ids = dict(
   314	            GroupShare.objects.filter(group__in=item_list).values_list("group_id", "uuid")
   315	        )
   316	
   317	        seen_stats = self._get_seen_stats(item_list, user)
   318	
   319	        organization_id_list = list({item.project.organization_id for item in item_list})
   320	        # if no groups, then we can't proceed but this seems to be a valid use case
   321	        if not item_list:
   322	            return {}
   323	        if len(organization_id_list) > 1:
   324	            # this should never happen but if it does we should know about it
   325	            logger.warning(
   326	                "Found multiple organizations for groups: %s, with orgs: %s",
   327	                [item.id for item in item_list],
   328	                organization_id_list,
   329	            )
   330	
   331	        # should only have 1 org at this point
   332	        organization_id = organization_id_list[0]
   333	
   334	        annotations_by_group_id: MutableMapping[int, list[Any]] = defaultdict(list)
   335	        for annotations_by_group in itertools.chain.from_iterable(
   336	            [
   337	                self._resolve_integration_annotations(organization_id, item_list),
   338	                [self._resolve_external_issue_annotations(item_list)],
   339	            ]
   340	        ):
   341	            merge_list_dictionaries(annotations_by_group_id, annotations_by_group)
   342	
   343	        snuba_stats = self._get_group_snuba_stats(item_list, seen_stats)
   344	
   345	        result = {}
   346	        for item in item_list:
   347	            active_date = item.active_at or item.first_seen
   348	
   349	            resolution_actor = None
   350	            resolution_type = None
   351	            resolution = release_resolutions.get(item.id)
   352	            if resolution:
   353	                resolution_type = "release"
   354	                resolution_actor = actors.get(resolution[-1])
   355	            if not resolution:
   356	                resolution = commit_resolutions.get(item.id)
   357	                if resolution:
   358	                    resolution_type = "commit"
   359	
   360	            ignore_item = ignore_items.get(item.id)
   361	
   362	            result[item] = {
   363	                "id": item.id,
   364	                "assigned_to": resolved_assignees.get(item.id),
   365	                "is_bookmarked": item.id in bookmarks,
   366	                "subscription": subscriptions[item.id],
   367	                "has_seen": seen_groups.get(item.id, active_date) > active_date,
   368	                "annotations": annotations_by_group_id[item.id],
   369	                "ignore_until": ignore_item,
   370	                "ignore_actor": actors.get(ignore_item.actor_id) if ignore_item else None,
   371	                "resolution": resolution,
   372	                "resolution_type": resolution_type,
   373	                "resolution_actor": resolution_actor,
   374	                "share_id": share_ids.get(item.id),
   375	            }
   376	            if snuba_stats is not None:
   377	                result[item]["is_unhandled"] = bool(snuba_stats.get(item.id, {}).get("unhandled"))
   378	
   379	            if seen_stats:
   380	                result[item].update(seen_stats.get(item, {}))
   381	        return result

   383	    def serialize(
   384	        self,
   385	        obj: Group,
   386	        attrs: Mapping[str, Any],
   387	        user: User | RpcUser | AnonymousUser,
   388	        **kwargs: Any,
   389	    ) -> BaseGroupSerializerResponse:
   390	        status_details, status_label = self._get_status(attrs, obj)
   391	        permalink = self._get_permalink(attrs, obj)
   392	        is_subscribed, subscription_details = get_subscription_from_attributes(attrs)
   393	        share_id = attrs["share_id"]
   394	        priority_label = PriorityLevel(obj.priority).to_str() if obj.priority else None
   395	        issue_category = obj.issue_category.name.lower()
   396	
   397	        group_dict: BaseGroupSerializerResponse = {
   398	            "id": str(obj.id),
   399	            "shareId": share_id,
   400	            "shortId": obj.qualified_short_id,
   401	            "title": obj.title,
   402	            "culprit": obj.culprit,
   403	            "permalink": permalink,
   404	            "logger": obj.logger or None,
   405	            "level": _get_level_label(obj),
   406	            "status": status_label,
   407	            "statusDetails": status_details,
   408	            "substatus": _get_substatus_label(obj),
   409	            "isPublic": share_id is not None,
   410	            "platform": obj.platform,
   411	            "project": _make_group_project_response(obj.project),
   412	            "type": obj.get_event_type(),
   413	            "metadata": obj.get_event_metadata(),
   414	            "numComments": obj.num_comments,
   415	            "assignedTo": serialize(attrs["assigned_to"], user, ActorSerializer()),
   416	            "isBookmarked": attrs["is_bookmarked"],
   417	            "isSubscribed": is_subscribed,
   418	            "subscriptionDetails": subscription_details,
   419	            "hasSeen": attrs["has_seen"],
   420	            "annotations": attrs["annotations"],
   421	            "issueType": obj.issue_type.slug,
   422	            "issueCategory": issue_category,
   423	            "priority": priority_label,
   424	            "priorityLockedAt": obj.priority_locked_at,
   425	            "seerFixabilityScore": obj.seer_fixability_score,
   426	            "seerAutofixLastTriggered": obj.seer_autofix_last_triggered,
   427	            "seerExplorerAutofixLastTriggered": obj.seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered,
   428	        }
   429	
   430	        # This attribute is currently feature gated
   431	        if "is_unhandled" in attrs:
   432	            group_dict["isUnhandled"] = attrs["is_unhandled"]
   433	        if is_seen_stats(attrs):
   434	            group_dict.update(self._convert_seen_stats(attrs))
   435	        return group_dict

   437	    @abstractmethod
   438	    def _seen_stats_error(
   439	        self, error_issue_list: Sequence[Group], user
   440	    ) -> Mapping[Group, SeenStats]:
   441	        pass
   442	
   443	    @abstractmethod
   444	    def _seen_stats_generic(
   445	        self, generic_issue_list: Sequence[Group], user
   446	    ) -> Mapping[Group, SeenStats]:
   447	        pass
   448	
   449	    def _expand(self, key) -> bool:
   450	        if self.expand is None:
   451	            return False
   452	
   453	        return key in self.expand
   454	
   455	    def _collapse(self, key) -> bool:
   456	        if self.collapse is None:
   457	            return False
   458	        return key in self.collapse
   459	
   460	    def _get_status(self, attrs: Mapping[str, Any], obj: Group):
   461	        status = obj.status
   462	        status_details = {}
   463	        if attrs["ignore_until"]:
   464	            snooze = attrs["ignore_until"]
   465	            if snooze.is_valid(group=obj):
   466	                # counts return the delta remaining when window is not set
   467	                status_details.update(
   468	                    {
   469	                        "ignoreCount": (
   470	                            snooze.count - (obj.times_seen - snooze.state["times_seen"])
   471	                            if snooze.count and not snooze.window
   472	                            else snooze.count
   473	                        ),
   474	                        "ignoreUntil": snooze.until,
   475	                        "ignoreUserCount": (
   476	                            snooze.user_count - (attrs["user_count"] - snooze.state["users_seen"])
   477	                            if snooze.user_count
   478	                            and not snooze.user_window
   479	                            and not self._collapse("stats")
   480	                            else snooze.user_count
   481	                        ),
   482	                        "ignoreUserWindow": snooze.user_window,
   483	                        "ignoreWindow": snooze.window,
   484	                        "actor": attrs["ignore_actor"],
   485	                    }
   486	                )
   487	            else:
   488	                status = GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED
   489	        # If the issue is UNRESOLVED but has resolved_at set, it means the user manually
   490	        # unresolved it after it was resolved. We should respect that and not override
   491	        # the status back to RESOLVED.
   492	        if status == GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED and obj.is_over_resolve_age() and not obj.resolved_at:
   493	            # When an issue is over the auto-resolve age but the task has not yet run
   494	            # Only show as auto-resolved if this group type has auto-resolve enabled
   495	            if obj.issue_type.enable_auto_resolve:
   496	                status = GroupStatus.RESOLVED
   497	                status_details["autoResolved"] = True
   498	        if status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   499	            status_label = "resolved"
   500	            if attrs["resolution_type"] == "release":
   501	                res_type, res_version, _ = attrs["resolution"]
   502	                if res_type in (GroupResolution.Type.in_next_release, None):
   503	                    status_details["inNextRelease"] = True
   504	                elif res_type == GroupResolution.Type.in_release:
   505	                    status_details["inRelease"] = res_version
   506	                status_details["actor"] = attrs["resolution_actor"]
   507	            elif attrs["resolution_type"] == "commit":
   508	                status_details["inCommit"] = attrs["resolution"]
   509	        elif status == GroupStatus.IGNORED:
   510	            status_label = "ignored"
   511	        elif status in [GroupStatus.PENDING_DELETION, GroupStatus.DELETION_IN_PROGRESS]:
   512	            status_label = "pending_deletion"
   513	        elif status == GroupStatus.PENDING_MERGE:
   514	            status_label = "pending_merge"
   515	        elif status == GroupStatus.REPROCESSING:
   516	            status_label = "reprocessing"
   517	            status_details["pendingEvents"], status_details["info"] = get_progress(
   518	                attrs["id"], obj.project.id
   519	            )
   520	        else:
   521	            status_label = "unresolved"
   522	        return status_details, status_label
   523	
   524	    def _get_seen_stats(self, item_list: Sequence[Group], user) -> Mapping[Group, SeenStats] | None:
   525	        """
   526	        Returns a dictionary keyed by item that includes:
   527	            - times_seen
   528	            - first_seen
   529	            - last_seen
   530	            - user_count
   531	        """
   532	        if self._collapse("stats"):
   533	            return None
   534	
   535	        if not item_list:
   536	            return None
   537	
   538	        # partition the item_list by type
   539	        error_issues = [group for group in item_list if GroupCategory.ERROR == group.issue_category]
   540	        generic_issues = [
   541	            group for group in item_list if group.issue_category != GroupCategory.ERROR
   542	        ]
   543	
   544	        # bulk query for the seen_stats by type
   545	        error_stats = (self._seen_stats_error(error_issues, user) if error_issues else {}) or {}
   546	        generic_stats = (
   547	            self._seen_stats_generic(generic_issues, user) if generic_issues else {}
   548	        ) or {}
   549	        agg_stats = {**error_stats, **generic_stats}
   550	        # combine results back
   551	        return {group: agg_stats[group] for group in item_list if group in agg_stats}
   552	
   553	    def _get_group_snuba_stats(
   554	        self, item_list: Sequence[Group], seen_stats: Mapping[Group, SeenStats] | None
   555	    ):
   556	        if self._collapse("unhandled") and len(item_list) > 0:
   557	            return None
   558	        start = self._get_start_from_seen_stats(seen_stats)
   559	        unhandled = {}
   560	
   561	        cache_keys = []
   562	        for item in item_list:
   563	            cache_keys.append(f"group-mechanism-handled:{item.id}")
   564	
   565	        cache_data = cache.get_many(cache_keys)
   566	        for item, cache_key in zip(item_list, cache_keys):
   567	            unhandled[item.id] = cache_data.get(cache_key)
   568	
   569	        filter_keys: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
   570	        for item in item_list:
   571	            if unhandled.get(item.id) is not None:
   572	                continue
   573	            filter_keys.setdefault("project_id", []).append(item.project_id)
   574	            filter_keys.setdefault("group_id", []).append(item.id)
   575	
   576	        if filter_keys:
   577	            rv = raw_query(
   578	                dataset=Dataset.Events,
   579	                selected_columns=[
   580	                    "group_id",
   581	                    [
   582	                        "argMax",
   583	                        [["has", ["exception_stacks.mechanism_handled", 0]], "timestamp"],
   584	                        "unhandled",
   585	                    ],
   586	                ],
   587	                groupby=["group_id"],
   588	                filter_keys=filter_keys,
   589	                start=start,
   590	                orderby="group_id",
   591	                referrer="group.unhandled-flag",
   592	                tenant_ids=(

   594	                ),

   598

   600	                # the time we give for buffer flushes so the user experience is somewhat
   601	                # consistent here.

   606	    @staticmethod
   607	    def _get_start_from_seen_stats(seen_stats: Mapping[Group, SeenStats] | None):
   608	        # Try to figure out what is a reasonable time frame to look into stats,
   609	        # based on a given "seen stats".  We try to pick a day prior to the earliest last seen,
   610	        # but it has to be at least 14 days, and not more than 90 days ago.
   611	        # Fallback to the 30 days ago if we are not able to calculate the value.
   612	        last_seen: datetime | None = None
   613	        if seen_stats:
   614	            for item in seen_stats.values():
   615	                if last_seen is None or (item["last_seen"] and last_seen > item["last_seen"]):
   616	                    last_seen = item["last_seen"]
   617	
   618	        if last_seen is None:
   619	            return datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=30)

I found Sentry’s own deletion registry for group-related models, which is a strong completeness anchor for persistent references. I’m using that as the backbone, then adding boundary-only dependents that do not own a table: search APIs, eventstore/snuba `issue.id`, notifications, integrations, workflow engine task payloads, reprocessing, and frontend consumers.

    44	class ActivityManager(BaseManager["Activity"]):
    45	    def get_activities_for_group(self, group: Group, num: int) -> Sequence[Activity]:
    46	        activities = []
    47	        activity_qs = self.filter(group=group).order_by("-datetime")
    48	
    49	        initial_priority_value = group.get_event_metadata().get("initial_priority")
    50	        initial_priority = (
    51	            PriorityLevel(initial_priority_value).to_str() if initial_priority_value else None
    52	        )
    53	
    54	        prev_sig = None
    55	        sig = None
    56	        # we select excess so we can filter dupes
    57	        for item in activity_qs[: num * 2]:
    58	            prev_sig = sig
    59	            sig = (item.type, item.ident, item.user_id, item.data)
    60	
    61	            if item.type == ActivityType.NOTE.value:
    62	                activities.append(item)
    63	                continue
    64	
    65	            if sig != prev_sig:
    66	                activities.append(item)
    67	
    68	        activities.append(
    69	            Activity(
    70	                id=0,
    71	                project=group.project,
    72	                group=group,
    73	                type=ActivityType.FIRST_SEEN.value,
    74	                data={"priority": initial_priority},
    75	                datetime=group.first_seen,
    76	            )
    77	        )
    78	
    79	        return activities[:num]

    81	    def create_group_activity(
    82	        self,
    83	        group: Group,
    84	        type: ActivityType,
    85	        user: User | RpcUser | None = None,
    86	        user_id: int | None = None,
    87	        data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    88	        send_notification: bool = True,
    89	        datetime: datetime | None = None,
    90	        detector_id: DetectorId | None = None,
    91	    ) -> Activity:
    92	        if user:
    93	            user_id = user.id
    94	        activity_args = {
    95	            "project_id": group.project_id,
    96	            "group": group,
    97	            "type": type.value,
    98	            "data": data,
    99	        }
   100	        if user_id is not None:
   101	            activity_args["user_id"] = user_id
   102	        if datetime is not None:
   103	            activity_args["datetime"] = datetime
   104	        activity = self.create(**activity_args)
   105	
   106	        if send_notification:
   107	            activity.send_notification()
   108	
   109	        invoke_workflow_activity_handlers(group, activity, detector_id)
   110	
   111	        return activity

   122	    ident = models.CharField(max_length=64, null=True)
   123	    # if the user is not set, it's assumed to be the system
   124	    user_id = HybridCloudForeignKey(settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL, null=True, on_delete="SET_NULL")
   125	    datetime = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
   126	    data = LegacyTextJSONField(default=dict, null=True)

   133	        indexes = (

   135	            models.Index(fields=("project", "type")),
   136	        )

   140	    @staticmethod
   141	    def get_version_ident(version: str | None) -> str:
   142	        return (version or "")[:64]
   143	
   144	    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
   145	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
   146	        from sentry.models.release import Release
   147	
   148	        # XXX(dcramer): fix for bad data
   149	        if self.type in (ActivityType.RELEASE.value, ActivityType.DEPLOY.value) and isinstance(
   150	            self.data["version"], Release
   151	        ):
   152	            self.data["version"] = self.data["version"].version
   153	        if self.type == ActivityType.ASSIGNED.value:
   154	            self.data["assignee"] = str(self.data["assignee"])
   155	
   156	    def save(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
   157	        created = bool(not self.id)
   158	
   159	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   160	
   161	        # The receiver for the post_save signal was not working in production, so just execute directly and safely
   162	        try:
   163	            from sentry.integrations.slack.tasks.send_notifications_on_activity import (
   164	                activity_created_receiver,
   165	            )
   166	
   167	            activity_created_receiver(self, created)
   168	        except Exception as err:
   169	            _default_logger.info(
   170	                "there was an error trying to kick off activity receiver",
   170	                "there was an error trying to kick off activity receiver",
   171	                exc_info=err,
   172	                extra={
   173	                    "activity_id": self.id,
   174	                },
   175	            )
   176	            pass
   177	
   178	        if not created:
   179	            return
   180	
   181	        # HACK: support Group.num_comments
   182	        if self.type == ActivityType.NOTE.value and self.group is not None:
   183	            from sentry.models.group import Group
   184	
   185	            self.group.update(num_comments=F("num_comments") + 1)
   186	            if not options.get("groups.enable-post-update-signal"):
   187	                post_save.send_robust(
   188	                    sender=Group, instance=self.group, created=True, update_fields=["num_comments"]
   189	                )
   190	
   191	    def delete(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> tuple[int, dict[str, int]]:
   192	        result = super().delete(*args, **kwargs)
   193	
   194	        # HACK: support Group.num_comments
   195	        if self.type == ActivityType.NOTE.value and self.group is not None:
   196	            from sentry.models.group import Group
   197	
   198	            self.group.update(num_comments=F("num_comments") - 1)
   199	            if not options.get("groups.enable-post-update-signal"):
   200	                post_save.send_robust(
   201	                    sender=Group, instance=self.group, created=True, update_fields=["num_comments"]
   202	                )
   203	
   204	        return result
   205	
   206	    def send_notification(self) -> None:
   207	        if self.group:
   208	            group_type = get_group_type_by_type_id(self.group.type)
   209	            has_status_change_notifications = group_type.enable_status_change_workflow_notifications
   210	            has_workflow_notifications = group_type.enable_workflow_notifications
   211	            is_status_change = self.type in {
   212	                activity.value for activity in STATUS_CHANGE_ACTIVITY_TYPES
   213	            }
   214	
   215	            # Skip sending the activity notification if the group type does not
   216	            # support status change workflow notifications
   217	            if (
   218	                is_status_change
   219	                and not has_status_change_notifications
   220	                or not has_workflow_notifications
   221	            ):
   222	                return
   223	
   224	        activity.send_activity_notifications.delay(self.id)
